reasoning: Classic structural refactor with well-defined before/after state and an explicit constraint that all 47 tests must pass unchanged. Low uncertainty (pattern is prescribed, not discovered). Cross-module blast radius (new files created, dispatcher changed). Moderate verifiability (tests confirm behavior preservation, but human review needed for design correctness). careful-refactor is optimal: it applies the Mikado method — characterize current behavior via tests, refactor incrementally, verify behavior preservation at each step.
